evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Implement appropriate dbt tests; Validate data outputs against business logic; Identify potential data quality issues early; Apply dimensional modeling; Monitor model runs
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour: testing, validation, quality identification, and monitoring dominate; no velocity/speed language.
evidence.domain_risk: payments/fintech; retail and hospitality; errors affect business operations without regulatory/financial reporting consequence
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Moderate: payments domain suggests some risk but JD is generic analytics infrastructure without explicit compliance/regulatory language.
evidence.collaboration_width: Senior Analytics Engineers; Data Analysts; Data Engineers; stakeholders
evidence.data_team_maturity: Contribute to established models; Apply dimensional modeling; Support analytics use cases; Contribute to shared team standards
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Mature: established dbt practices, dimensional modeling, shared standards, and mentoring structures signal mature function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Independently develop dbt models; Apply dimensional modeling; Implement dbt tests; Validate data outputs; Refactor existing models; Monitor model runs and respond to failures
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Hiring manager: specific technical ownership (dbt development, testing, validation, performance) and data layer responsibilities show domain expertise.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: analysts; analytics use cases; reporting requirements; BI consumption patterns
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Internal data: role focuses on data infrastructure supporting analytics consumers and BI platforms.
evidence.autonomy_level: Independently develop; Contribute under guidance; Work closely with analysts; Own documentation; Monitor runs and respond
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Execution: candidate owns defined dbt model development and testing within senior AE guidance and analyst collaboration.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools or expected AI capability.
evidence.testing_framing: Implement appropriate dbt tests; Validate data outputs against business logic; Identify potential data quality issues early
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Responsibility: testing and validation ownership framed as core responsibility the candidate accountable for.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Identify potential data quality issues early; Monitor model runs and respond to failures or anomalies; Be mindful of model performance and warehouse costs
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: data quality and reliability concern secondary to analytics model delivery and cost efficiency.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Develop; Refactor existing models; Contribute to models; Improve clarity, performance, or consistency
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Fix/scale: improving and extending existing analytics models and infrastructure.
evidence.loss_aversion: Implement appropriate dbt tests; Validate data outputs; Identify potential data quality issues early; Monitor model runs and respond to failures.
